prerequisites: A Taboola Backstage advertiser or publisher account with API access enabled.
An account_id: your Taboola account identifier (a unique alphabetic string, e.g. "taboola-demo-account"), provided during API onboarding or retrievable via the users/current/account endpoint.
A client_id and client_secret OAuth2 application credential pair, provided by your Taboola Account Manager during onboarding.

authMethods.name: OAuth2 client credentials
authMethods.summary: Taboola Backstage uses OAuth2 (RFC 6749) with the client credentials flow. pm submits your client_id and client_secret to the token endpoint (https://backstage.taboola.com/backstage/oauth/token) with grant_type=client_credentials and receives a Bearer access token, which is sent in the Authorization header of each API request. Tokens are valid for 12 hours (43200 seconds) and no refresh token is issued, so pm re-authenticates with the client_id/client_secret when the token expires.

setupSteps.title: Request API credentials from your Taboola Account Manager
setupSteps.body: Taboola Backstage API credentials are not self-service. Ask your Taboola Account Manager to provision an API client and provide you with a client_id and client_secret. Keep the client_secret confidential.

setupSteps.title: Find your account_id
setupSteps.body: Your account_id is the unique identifier for the Taboola account you want to read from. It is a unique alphabetic string that may contain dashes but no spaces or underscores (for example "taboola-demo-account"). It is supplied during onboarding, or you can retrieve it from the Backstage API users/current/account endpoint. The account_id is used as a path component in Backstage API requests.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter account_id, client_id, and client_secret (all required) in the source-taboola connector configuration. pm exchanges the client_id and client_secret for an access token at https://backstage.taboola.com/backstage/oauth/token using grant_type=client_credentials, then calls the Backstage API for the given account_id.
